project: Adult Player Lifecycle — Phase 4: Daily Player Wellness Check
description: Adds a quick emoji-based daily wellness check across 5 dimensions (physical, energy, mental, muscle recovery, motivation) for adult and youth players. Includes GDPR-compliant optional menstrual cycle phase tracking for female players (GDPR Article 9 special category data — explicit consent required). Adds coach team wellness dashboard, admin wellness/injury correlation analytics, under-18 parent visibility, and admin-configurable reminders.
readinessLevel: 100% - Schema design complete, gender field on playerIdentities confirmed, Resend email infrastructure live

criticalContext: NEW TABLES REQUIRED: dailyPlayerHealthChecks and playerHealthConsents — add both to packages/backend/convex/schema.ts before any frontend work

dailyPlayerHealthChecks schema: playerIdentityId (id), organizationId (string), checkDate (string 'YYYY-MM-DD'), physicalFeeling/energyLevel/mentalWellbeing/muscleRecovery/motivation (all number 1-5), cyclePhase (optional union literal), notes (optional string), submittedAt (number), updatedAt (number). Indexes: by_player_and_date(['playerIdentityId','checkDate']), by_org_and_date(['organizationId','checkDate']), by_player(['playerIdentityId'])

playerHealthConsents schema: playerIdentityId (id), consentType (literal 'cycle_tracking'), givenAt (number), withdrawnAt (optional number). Index: by_player_and_type(['playerIdentityId','consentType'])

GDPR CRITICAL: Menstrual cycle data = GDPR Article 9 Special Category health data. Cycle phase section MUST NOT appear without prior explicit consent. Coaches MUST NEVER see cycle phase data — enforce at query level, not just UI.
gender field on playerIdentities: v.union(v.literal('male'), v.literal('female'), v.literal('other')) — use to conditionally show cycle phase section
Wellness rating scale: 1=😢 Very Poor (#ef4444), 2=😕 Poor (#f97316), 3=😐 Neutral (#eab308), 4=🙂 Good (#86efac), 5=😁 Great (#22c55e)
Recharts already installed — use LineChart, BarChart, ResponsiveContainer for analytics charts
CSV export pattern: Blob + URL.createObjectURL + link.download. Escape commas: value.replace(/,/g, ';')
Coach wellness query getTeamWellnessSummary MUST NOT include cyclePhase in return type — hard privacy requirement enforced at backend
Under-18 players: cycle phase section hidden entirely (cannot consent to Article 9 data without parental consent — simplest approach: hide for youth playerType)
Org settings storage: find existing pattern for storing org-level config before implementing reminder settings
Always use .withIndex() — NEVER .filter(). All queries/mutations must include returns validator.
keyFiles: packages/backend/convex/schema.ts — add dailyPlayerHealthChecks and playerHealthConsents tables
packages/backend/convex/models/playerHealthChecks.ts — NEW file: all wellness check queries and mutations
apps/web/src/app/orgs/[orgId]/player/page.tsx — existing overview (extend with wellness CTA — may already be done in Phase 1)
apps/web/src/app/orgs/[orgId]/parents/ — parent portal (add Wellness read-only tab for under-18)
packages/backend/convex/jobs/ — wellness reminder cron job goes here

criticalPatterns.CyclePhaseGDPR: NEVER show cycle phase input without prior getCycleTrackingConsent check returning a non-withdrawn consent record. First interaction must intercept with consent modal. Consent modal checkbox NOT pre-ticked.
criticalPatterns.CoachNeverSeesCyclePhase: getTeamWellnessSummary return type must not include cyclePhase field. This is enforced at the Convex query level, not just filtered on frontend.
criticalPatterns.ConsentWithdrawal: withdrawCycleTrackingConsent must: (1) set withdrawnAt on consent record AND (2) null/delete all cyclePhase values from past health check records for that player.
criticalPatterns.Under18CycleHidden: If playerType === 'youth', do not render cycle phase section at all. No GDPR consent flow for under-18.
criticalPatterns.RechartsPattern: Always wrap charts in <ResponsiveContainer width='100%' height={300}>. Use grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 for responsive layout.
criticalPatterns.MobileTouchTargets: Emoji buttons must be minimum 44×44px touch target on mobile.
criticalPatterns.IndexUsage: Use by_player_and_date for single-player queries, by_org_and_date for org-wide, by_player for history ranges.

userStories.title: Wellness Check Schema & Backend Functions
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I need the schema and core CRUD functions for daily wellness checks, so that all Phase 4 frontend stories have a backend to work with.
acceptanceCriteria: Add dailyPlayerHealthChecks table to packages/backend/convex/schema.ts with all fields and indexes as specified in context above
Add playerHealthConsents table to schema.ts with fields and index as specified in context above
Create packages/backend/convex/models/playerHealthChecks.ts with these functions:

submitDailyHealthCheck mutation: args (playerIdentityId, organizationId, checkDate, physicalFeeling, energyLevel, mentalWellbeing, muscleRecovery, motivation, cyclePhase?, notes?), check for existing record on same date via by_player_and_date, throw if already exists with message 'already submitted today — use updateDailyHealthCheck', insert new record
updateDailyHealthCheck mutation: args (recordId plus same fields as submit), patch existing record, update updatedAt
getTodayHealthCheck query: args (playerIdentityId, checkDate), returns record or null via by_player_and_date index
getMyHealthChecks query: args (playerIdentityId, startDate, endDate), returns array of records in date range via by_player index

getTeamWellnessSummary query: args (teamId, checkDate, organizationId), batch fetch players on team, batch fetch their health checks for that date, return per-player {playerIdentityId, playerName, avgScore, scores: {physicalFeeling, energyLevel, mentalWellbeing, muscleRecovery, motivation}, hasAlert: boolean} — cyclePhase MUST NOT be in return type
getOrgWellnessAnalytics query: args (organizationId, startDate, endDate), returns team-level daily averages and correlation data (players with 3+ consecutive days avg < 3 overlapping with injuries)
grantCycleTrackingConsent mutation: args (playerIdentityId), insert into playerHealthConsents
withdrawCycleTrackingConsent mutation: args (playerIdentityId), set withdrawnAt on consent record AND patch all that player's health check records to remove cyclePhase (set to undefined)
getCycleTrackingConsent query: args (playerIdentityId), returns consent record or null
All functions include returns validators with full shape
npx -w packages/backend convex codegen passes
npm run check-types passes
userStories.notes: This is the foundational story for all Phase 4 frontend stories — must be completed first. The getTeamWellnessSummary return type is a hard requirement: cyclePhase must never be returned to coaches.

userStories.title: Daily Wellness Check Submission UI (Player)
userStories.description: As an adult player, I want to quickly rate my wellness each day using emojis, so that my coaches and club have visibility into my readiness and wellbeing.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/web/src/app/orgs/[orgId]/player/health-check/page.tsx
Show all 5 questions on one scrollable screen for speed
Questions: 'How does your body feel?', 'How is your energy/sleep?', 'How are you feeling mentally?', 'How are your muscles/soreness?', 'How motivated are you for training today?'
Each question has 5 emoji buttons: 😢 (1 Very Poor, red #ef4444), 😕 (2 Poor, orange #f97316), 😐 (3 Neutral, yellow #eab308), 🙂 (4 Good, light green #86efac), 😁 (5 Great, green #22c55e)
Each emoji button minimum 44×44px touch target for mobile
Selected emoji highlights with its accent colour background
Submit button disabled until all 5 core questions answered
Female players only (player.gender === 'female' AND playerType === 'adult'): show cycle phase section below 5 questions with 'Optional — You don't have to answer this' label
Cycle phase: 5 horizontal pills: 'Menstruation (Days 1-5)', 'Early Follicular (Days 6-9)', 'Ovulation (Days 10-14)', 'Early Luteal (Days 15-21)', 'Late Luteal (Days 22-28)'
First tap on any cycle phase pill: check getCycleTrackingConsent — if no consent, intercept with GDPR consent modal (US-P4-004) before recording selection
On submit: calls submitDailyHealthCheck mutation
If today's check already exists (getTodayHealthCheck returns record): show existing answers pre-selected, submit button becomes 'Update', calls updateDailyHealthCheck
Success toast: 'Wellness check submitted ✓'
Streak indicator below submit: 'You've checked in X days in a row 🔥' — computed from getMyHealthChecks consecutive days
Mobile-first layout at 375px minimum
npm run check-types passes
userStories.notes: US-P4-001 must be complete first. The GDPR consent modal (US-P4-004) is a dependency for cycle phase — if not yet built, stub the check as a TODO comment and skip showing cycle phase section.

userStories.title: Under-18 Player Health Check Access
userStories.description: As a youth player with their own account, I want to submit my daily wellness check, so that my parents and coaches can monitor my wellbeing.
acceptanceCriteria: The health-check page is accessible from the player portal for both adult AND youth players who have claimed their account
Add a 'Youth Account' badge visible in the player portal layout header for users with playerType === 'youth'
Parent must explicitly enable under-18 player portal access: add a toggle in parent portal settings per child — 'Allow [Name] to access their own player portal'
If parent has NOT enabled access: youth player sees the player portal with a 'Your parent hasn't enabled your player access yet' message instead of content
Parent Wellness read-only tab: add 'Wellness' tab to the child's detail view in parent portal, showing their check-in history with dates and 5 scores — read-only, no submit controls
Parent does NOT receive any alerts or notifications based on wellness scores
When player transitions to adult (playerType changes to 'adult'): parent access to wellness data is automatically blocked by the query's playerType check
Cycle phase section DOES NOT appear for youth players (playerType === 'youth') — hide entirely, no GDPR flow for under-18
npm run check-types passes
userStories.notes: Find the parent portal settings page to add the per-child toggle. The parent Wellness tab uses getMyHealthChecks scoped to the child's playerIdentityId.

userStories.title: GDPR Consent Flow for Menstrual Cycle Tracking
userStories.description: As a female player, I want to be clearly informed about what menstrual cycle data is collected and give explicit consent, so that my sensitive health data is handled in accordance with GDPR.
acceptanceCriteria: Before a female player can interact with cycle phase section, check getCycleTrackingConsent — if consent exists with no withdrawnAt, show section directly; otherwise intercept with consent modal
Consent modal title: 'Menstrual Cycle Tracking'

Consent modal body (plain language): what is collected (cycle phase only, not dates), why (sports performance and injury risk analysis), who sees it (you and authorised medical staff at [org] only — coaches do NOT see this), how long kept (up to 18 months), and that consent can be withdrawn at any time
Include link to PlayerARC privacy policy in the modal
Single explicit opt-in checkbox: 'I consent to PlayerARC storing my menstrual cycle phase data for sports performance analysis. I can withdraw this consent at any time in Settings.'
Checkbox is NOT pre-ticked
Two buttons: 'I Consent' (enabled only when checkbox ticked, calls grantCycleTrackingConsent) and 'Skip / No Thanks' (hides cycle section, stores preference, no consent record created)
Create apps/web/src/app/orgs/[orgId]/player/settings/page.tsx if it doesn't exist
In player settings: 'Menstrual Cycle Tracking' toggle showing current consent status
Toggling OFF calls withdrawCycleTrackingConsent — sets withdrawnAt AND deletes all cyclePhase values from past health check records
Toggling ON shows the consent modal again
npm run check-types passes
userStories.notes: GDPR Article 9 Special Category data. Consent must be separate, explicit, not pre-ticked, and withdrawable with data deletion. US-P4-001 (grantCycleTrackingConsent and withdrawCycleTrackingConsent mutations) must be built first.

userStories.title: Coach Team Wellness Dashboard
userStories.description: As a coach, I want to see my players' daily wellness scores, so that I can adapt training and identify players who need support.
acceptanceCriteria: Add 'Team Wellness' section to coach Team Hub — extend the existing health-safety-widget.tsx pattern (find this file in the coach pages)
Section header: today's check-in completion rate — 'X/Y players checked in today'
Player list: each player's name + average wellness score (avg of 5 dimensions) as colour-coded badge: red (avg < 2), orange (avg 2-2.9), yellow (avg 3-3.9), green (avg >= 4)
Players with ANY single dimension score <= 2 get a ⚠️ alert icon next to their name
Tapping a player row expands to show individual 5-dimension breakdown for today
7-day rolling wellness trend: small Recharts sparkline or bar chart per player (use ResponsiveContainer)
CYCLE PHASE IS NEVER SHOWN TO COACHES — the getTeamWellnessSummary query does not return it, and no UI element reveals it
Empty state: 'No wellness check-ins yet today' when no submissions
Data fetched via getTeamWellnessSummary(teamId, checkDate, organizationId)
npm run check-types passes
userStories.notes: Find health-safety-widget.tsx in the coach pages to understand the extension point. US-P4-001 (getTeamWellnessSummary) must be complete. The backend query enforces the cycle phase privacy — trust it.

userStories.title: Admin Wellness Reminder Configuration
userStories.description: As an org admin, I want to configure wellness check reminders for my organisation, so that players are prompted to check in at the right time.
acceptanceCriteria: Add 'Wellness Reminders' section to org admin settings page
Master toggle: 'Enable wellness check reminders' — when off, no reminders sent
When enabled: frequency options (radio buttons): 'Daily', 'Match days only', 'Training days only'
When enabled: reminder type checkboxes: 'In-app notification', 'Email', or both
Reminders only sent to players who haven't submitted a check for today (use by_player_and_date index)
Separate 'Low Score Alerts' section: toggle to enable admin/medical staff alerts when a player scores <= 2 on any dimension
Low score alert recipient options: 'Org admins', 'Medical staff only', 'Both'
Low score alerts NEVER sent to coaches
Store reminder settings in org settings — find the existing org settings storage pattern before implementing
Reminder sending: scheduled Convex cron job or action triggered by mutation — logic in packages/backend/convex/jobs/ directory
npm run check-types passes
userStories.notes: Find how org-level settings are stored (likely organization record or separate orgSettings table). Follow that pattern. Reminder cron goes in packages/backend/convex/jobs/ matching existing cron patterns.

userStories.title: Admin Wellness & Injury Correlation Analytics
userStories.description: As an org admin or medical staff member, I want to see wellness data alongside injury records, so that I can identify patterns and reduce injury risk.
acceptanceCriteria: Add 'Player Wellness' tab or section to admin analytics — add to existing admin analytics page or create new page if analytics doesn't exist yet
Team-level view: average daily wellness scores per team as Recharts LineChart time series (ResponsiveContainer, grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 layout)
Correlation view: list of players with 3+ consecutive days avg wellness score < 3, alongside any injuries reported in that period — informational, not alerting
Correlation view shows: player name, low wellness period (start/end dates), any injury records in that period

Cycle phase analysis section (visible only to admins with medical access): aggregate chart of injury occurrences grouped by cycle phase across all female players in org — labelled 'Medical Staff Access Only'
Date range filter: start and end date pickers, affects all charts and tables
CSV export: exports wellness data for selected date range — player name, date, 5 dimension scores, cycle phase (only if viewer has medical access)
CSV export: escape commas in values (replace with semicolons), use Blob + URL.createObjectURL + link.download
Data fetched via getOrgWellnessAnalytics(organizationId, startDate, endDate)
npm run check-types passes
userStories.notes: US-P4-001 (getOrgWellnessAnalytics) must be complete. Follow Recharts patterns from existing analytics pages. Check if an admin analytics page already exists before creating a new one.

userStories.title: Phase 4 Daily Wellness E2E Tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Playwright E2E tests for the daily wellness check feature, so that submission, editing, visibility rules, and GDPR consent all work correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Create test file: apps/web/uat/tests/daily-wellness-phase4.spec.ts
Test: wellness check page shows 5 questions with 5 emoji buttons each
Test: submit button disabled until all 5 answered
Test: submit with all 5 answered shows success toast and record created in DB
Test: revisiting same day shows existing answers pre-selected and 'Update' button
Test: cycle phase section hidden for male player
Test: GDPR consent modal appears on first cycle phase interaction for female player (checkbox not pre-ticked)
Test: cycle phase section hidden/absent when consent withdrawn
Test: coach wellness dashboard shows today's completion count
Test: cycle phase data does NOT appear anywhere in coach view
Manual test 1: Log in as adult female player → navigate to Daily Wellness → confirm 5 questions with emoji buttons
Manual test 2: Tap emoji for each question → confirm selected highlights in colour
Manual test 3: Submit all 5 → confirm success toast and record in DB
Manual test 4: Return same day → confirm current answers shown, 'Update' button visible
Manual test 5: Edit one answer → update → confirm new answer persisted
Manual test 6: Try submit with only 4 answered → confirm submit button disabled
Manual test 7: Female player → scroll to cycle phase → confirm 'Optional' label visible
Manual test 8: First tap on cycle phase → confirm GDPR consent modal with unticked checkbox
Manual test 9: Accept consent → select phase → submit → confirm cyclePhase saved in DB
Manual test 10: Settings → toggle off cycle tracking → confirm withdrawnAt set and cyclePhase fields cleared from past records
Manual test 11: Log in as male player → confirm cycle phase section NOT shown
Manual test 12: Log in as coach → Team Wellness widget shows today's completion rate and player wellness badges
Manual test 13: Confirm coaches see NO cycle phase data anywhere
Manual test 14: Log in as org admin → navigate to wellness analytics → confirm org-wide charts visible
Manual test 15: Under-18 player (parent enabled access): submit wellness → log in as parent → confirm wellness visible read-only in parent portal
Manual test 16: Disable wellness reminders in admin settings → enable with daily frequency → confirm in-app notification sent to players
userStories.notes: All Phase 4 stories (US-P4-001 through US-P4-007) must be complete before this UAT story.
